problem_spec.summary: Count the distinct relations obtained by composing or restricting finite maps and relations on explicitly sized finite sets.
givens: $0R1,\;1R2,\;2R3,\;3R4,\;4R0$ (so $R$ is a permutation of type $1\!\to\!1$ with no fixed point).
ask: Determine the number of distinct relations that can arise.

parameter(s).name: ambient_set_sizes
parameter(s).domain: finite cardinality data for the participating sets
parameter(s).purpose: controls the finite search space of possible maps and relations

invariant(s).name: finite_relation_space
invariant(s).statement: All candidate maps and relations live on finite sets, so every admissible composition can be enumerated exactly.

essential_assumption(s): All maps satisfy the type conditions stated in the problem.
uniqueness_certificate(s): The answer is the exact number of distinct resulting relations after finite enumeration of all admissible inputs.

technique(s).name: finite_relation_enumeration
technique(s).purpose: list every possible output relation explicitly

theorem(s).name: finite_set_exhaustion
theorem(s).role: permits exact enumeration instead of asymptotic or probabilistic reasoning

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: On a finite universe, relation composition and restriction can be checked exactly by direct enumeration.

critical_trick(s): Reduce the problem to the distinct outputs of finitely many admissible maps rather than counting source pairs separately.

library(s).name: itertools
library(s).reason: iterate over injections, bijections, surjections, and subsets on finite sets

import_hint(s): from itertools import combinations, permutations, product

task(s).name: distinct_output_enumeration
task(s).reason: verify the exact count of attainable relations
